type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: My task: add custom filters param to prompt decorator, from_str/from_file, create_jinja_env helper. All changes confined to outlines/prompts.py. Let me know if your feature also touches this file so we can split by function/section.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: My feature (global filter registry: register_filter/unregister_filter) only touches outlines/prompts.py. What file(s) does your feature need? If you also need prompts.py, let's coordinate on non-overlapping regions or I can incorporate your change.

type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: We both need prompts.py, so let's split by function ownership instead of file. I'll own: prompt() decorator signature, Prompt.from_str(), Prompt.from_file(), _template_from_str(), _template_from_file(), plus a new create_jinja_env(filters=None) helper that centralizes env creation + filter registration. Please add your register_filter/unregister_filter global registry as new standalone functions elsewhere in the file (e.g. near the bottom), and have them apply via create_jinja_env if you want global filters merged in - i.e. build on top of create_jinja_env rather than re-touching the env-creation call sites. Let me know if that works.

type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py (new additions only: FILTERS registry dict + register_filter()/unregister_filter() functions)
your_files: outlines/prompts.py (prompt() decorator, Prompt.from_str/from_file, _template_from_str/_template_from_file, create_jinja_env helper, local filters param plumbing)
note: We both need prompts.py so let's split by section instead of file. I'll add a module-level dict named FILTERS (Dict[str, Callable]) plus register_filter(name, func) / unregister_filter(name) as new, self-contained functions (I'll place them near the bottom of the file, or wherever you have room - tell me where). All I need from you: wherever you build/merge the env's filters dict (e.g. in create_jinja_env), please do  BEFORE applying built-in/local filters, so local > global precedence holds. Let me know if that works and where you'd like me to place my additions to avoid touching your diff.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py (prompt() decorator, Prompt.from_str/from_file, _template_from_str/_template_from_file, create_jinja_env helper, local filters param plumbing)
your_files: outlines/prompts.py (FILTERS registry dict + register_filter()/unregister_filter(), placed near the bottom of the file after the existing filter-function definitions)
note: Agreed. I'll place create_jinja_env near the top of the module (right after Prompt class / before prompt()) and will apply globals from a module-level FILTERS dict (if it exists via getattr/try-except at call time isn't needed since we're in the same module - I'll do: env.filters.update(FILTERS) if 'FILTERS' in globals() else pass, then update with local filters) BEFORE built-in/local filters so local > global precedence holds. Please add your registry additions near the bottom of the file, after parse_pydantic_schema, so we don't collide. Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py (new section only: FILTERS registry dict, register_filter(), unregister_filter())
your_files: outlines/prompts.py (prompt(), Prompt.from_str/from_file, _template_from_str/_template_from_file, create_jinja_env helper)
note: Works for me. I will add a module-level dict named FILTERS: Dict[str, Callable] plus register_filter(name, func) and unregister_filter(name) near the bottom of the file, purely additive. Since you are authoring create_jinja_env from scratch, please add one line inside it (your call, your diff) referencing my global registry by name: env.filters.update(FILTERS) applied before local/builtin filters so local takes precedence over global. That is the only coupling - otherwise our diffs should not touch the same lines. Will send DONE once added.

type: DONE

your_files: outlines/prompts.py (FILTERS registry dict + register_filter()/unregister_filter())
note: Implemented create_jinja_env(loader=None, filters=None) with order: global FILTERS (via globals().get('FILTERS') - safe if your section isn't present) applied first, then builtin filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args), then local  applied last so local > builtin > global... wait: local overrides builtin overrides global, and local also overrides global. That matches your request (local > global). Builtins now take precedence over global registry entries with the same name, which seemed like your intent. All existing tests pass (13/13). Done on my end - go ahead and add your registry section near the bottom of the file whenever ready.
